title: Training Techniques Path
description: Build fluency in Training Techniques inside Neural Networks by moving from foundational terms into adjacent methods, variants, and extensions.

steps.title: Regularization Techniques
steps.summary: Techniques used to prevent overfitting, such as L1 and L2 regularization, dropout, and early stopping. It sits inside Neural Networks / Training Techniques. Start after Regularization and ℓ1 Regularization. It opens into Graph Regularization Techniques and Advanced Regularization Techniques.
steps.whyIncluded: Start here to anchor the vocabulary and core intuition for this area.

steps.title: Adversarial Training
steps.summary: Training a model to be robust to adversarial attacks. It sits inside Neural Networks / Training Techniques. Start after Model Training. It opens into PGD adversarial training and Adversarial Training (PGD).
steps.whyIncluded: Start here to anchor the vocabulary and core intuition for this area.

steps.title: Adversarial Training (PGD)
steps.summary: ** Adversarial Training (PGD) belongs to the Neural Networks area of AI/ML, within Training Techniques. It sits inside Neural Networks / Training Techniques. Start after Adversarial Training and Model Training. It opens into Adversarial Training (FGSM, PGD).
steps.whyIncluded: Use this after the foundations to connect patterns, variants, and system behavior.

steps.title: Adversarial Training (FGSM)
steps.summary: ** Adversarial Training (FGSM) belongs to the Neural Networks area of AI/ML, within Training Techniques. It sits inside Neural Networks / Training Techniques. Start after Adversarial Training and Model Training. It opens into Adversarial Training (FGSM, PGD).
steps.whyIncluded: Use this after the foundations to connect patterns, variants, and system behavior.

steps.title: Adversarial Training (FGSM, PGD)
steps.summary: ** Adversarial Training (FGSM, PGD) belongs to the Neural Networks area of AI/ML, within Training Techniques. It sits inside Neural Networks / Training Techniques. Start after PGD adversarial training and Adversarial Training (PGD).
steps.whyIncluded: Use this after the foundations to connect patterns, variants, and system behavior.

steps.title: Adaptive Regularization of Weights (AROW)
steps.summary: ** Adaptive Regularization of Weights (AROW) belongs to the Neural Networks area of AI/ML, within Training Techniques. It sits inside Neural Networks / Training Techniques. Start after Regularization and ℓ1 Regularization.
steps.whyIncluded: Treat this as an advanced extension once the earlier terms feel natural.

steps.title: Atlas pretraining (data engine)
steps.summary: ** Atlas pretraining (data engine) belongs to the Neural Networks area of AI/ML, within Training Techniques. It sits inside Neural Networks / Training Techniques. Start after Pretraining and Atlas.
